item.title: About Me
item.description: About this website: I made this website using Hugo and the Template “Blist”. I hosted it on Github Pages!

item.title: Handwriting Recognition Model
item.description: This model identifies handwritten words. It focuses on training a CRNN (CNN+RNN) model and uses the CTC (Connectionist Temporal Classification) loss.

item.title: Video Classification Model for Mice Responses to Dynamic Stimuli
item.description: Mathematical notation in a Hugo project can be enabled by using third party JavaScript libraries.

item.title: Red & Black Newspaper Website
item.description:
LINK TO WEBSITE: https://whsnewspaper.azurewebsites.net/about.html

Our school’s newspaper, The Red & Black, has over 20 articles a month written by various students. I am the website manager, using SQL and Javascript to put all the articles on a database which is then displayed on the website. I do this monthly, and enjoy the process.

item.title: Violin and Piano Duets
item.description: I love to play duets with myself, as I can play both violin and piano. I’ve been doing this for 3 years, and love the process every time. I find a song that is meaningful to me, and then I find sheet music for both violin and piano. Sometimes if there isn’t an existing part for violin, I have to compose my own. I did this for my “The Second Waltz” duet.

item.title: NHD Website
item.description: This is the Link to the website: https://site.nhd.org/74595508/home Our Thesis: The Treaty of Nanjing marked the end of the Opium war after Britain’s military overpowered China in 1842. Further pressure from Britain resulted in difficulties for Chinese diplomats attempting to negotiate fair terms. Misinterpretations of the treaty along with the emperor’s ineptitude hindered further negotiations after the first draft of the treaty. These factors led to major losses for China and marked the beginning of a century of mistreatment from other imperial powers.

item.title: Google Colabs
item.description: I lead our school’s AI Club. Our weekly meetings consist of various things, one of them being Google Colab lectures teaching Introductory Machine Learning topics. Here are a few that I have created. Open in Google Colab
